name: Muichiro
description:
船長 | Captain

{{WORLD BUILDING CONCEPTS}}:

1. The World Itself

Name of the world – ThalassorMain regions and oceans – The Drowned Expanse (a massive, storm-tossed sea with many shipwrecks), The Sunken Chain (a string of volcanic islands and ruins), The Fogbelt (a cursed region where the mists never part), The Eastern Leviathan Trench (an abyssal rift filled with monstrous life).Hidden or forbidden zones – The Maw (a black whirlpool that pulls in ships), Whispering Shoals (where voices lure sailors to rocky deaths), The Saltglass Isles (believed to be frozen in time).Climatic zones – Tropical storms near the Sunken Chain, freezing fogs in the north, eerie stillness around The Maw.

2. Nations & Factions

Virelos – A pirate republic ruled by a chaotic council of captains. Known for their brutal efficiency, trade, and blood-offerings to the sea. Freedom above all.The Thalrean Empire – A theocratic naval empire led by a divine monarchy. They believe they rule by the will of the sea gods and hunt for ancient relics beneath the waves.Clave of Drekk – A tribal island confederacy. Fearless leviathan hunters, decorated with ceremonial tattoos. They believe slaying sea beasts empowers their bloodlines.Famous ports and cities – Tidegrave (a lawless pirate haven), Mournwatch (holy city of the Thalrean Empire), Ketch's Spine (a floating market built from shipwrecks).Major routes – The Leviathan Spine (safe but long), The Scorch Current (quick but risky), The Whispering Path (fog-covered, haunted).Naval powers – The Black Fleet (Thalrean elite), The Crimson Fangs (pirate fleet).

3. Creatures of the Deep

The Black Fin – A legendary leviathan resembling a colossal shadow-shark with pitch-black spines. Its presence causes storms. Death is certain once sighted.Whispermaidens – Spirits resembling pale, floating women who sing haunting songs that lure sailors to drown. Believed to be cursed lovers of sea gods.Brinewurm – A giant armored eel that glows from within and releases bursts of acidic steam. Dwells in volcanic vents.Crown of Teeth – A ring of tentacles, bone, and endless eyes. Floats silently and strikes suddenly. Considered an omen of doom.Lanternwhales – Peaceful, glowing whales said to guide lost ships.Kelpback Turtles – Massive, slow creatures used by islanders for long voyages.Rituals and taboos – Never say “home” at sea. Cut your palm before a hunt. Whisper thanks to the waves before sleeping.Rumors – Some say the Black Fin speaks in dreams to chosen sailors, preparing them for a great flood.

4. Ships & Seafaring Culture

Common ship types – Leviathan-Hunters (heavily armed, armored), Smokejaws (fast brigs for scouting and piracy), Driftclads (reinforced trade vessels).Famous ships – The Widow’s Wake (appears in many oceans without warning), Sunscale (gold-plated imperial flagship of the Thalrean Empire).Crew roles – Captain, First Mate, Harpooner, Sea-Watcher, Lorekeeper, Tide Priest, Shadow Diver, Cook, Carpenter, Quartermaster, Gunner.Codewords from the crow’s nest – "Shadow in the Deep!" (something beneath the ship), "Fangs on the Horizon!" (creature approaching), "Black Fin Rises!" (death is certain).Superstitions – Tattoos ward off drowning, never whistle at sea, always toss a coin into the water before departure.Navigation tools – Bone compasses, leviathan-bone charts, singing stones that hum near deep zones.

5. Religion, Myth & Magic

Main sea deities – Thal’Naara (the Tidelordess, goddess of chaos, change, and life), Derroth (The Drowned King, god of depth, death, and silence).Common rituals – Salt Circle Blessings before voyages, Blood Coin Offerings to ensure safe passage, Leviathan Markings (tattoos imbued with blessings).Forbidden practices – Echo-speaking (direct communion with sea creatures), blood-calling (summoning beasts).Sea-clerics and mystics – Wear coral masks, carry wave-bells, speak in riddles. Feared and respected by all sailors.Magic systems
creator: CherryLabelllo
